title: Best Solar Plexus Chakra Crystals: Stones for Balance, Energy & Mindful Rituals

content:
Quick Answer: Best Crystals for the Solar Plexus Chakra

The best crystals for the Solar Plexus Chakra include Tiger Eye, Carnelian, Citrine, traditionally used in mindfulness and spiritual practices. Crystal properties are complementary wellness tools, not medical treatments.

Understanding the Solar Plexus Chakra

The Solar Plexus Chakra, called Manipura in Sanskrit, sits at the upper abdomen and is often described in yogic and energy traditions as the body's center of personal power. The Sanskrit name translates roughly to "city of jewels," a reminder that this area was long associated with an inner brightness, will, and selfhood. Where the Root Chakra asks for safety and the Sacral Chakra asks for feeling, the Solar Plexus asks a different question: what do I want, and do I have the will to move toward it?

In many Eastern traditions, this center is linked to the fire element and to agni, the digestive fire that transforms what we take in, whether food, experience, or intention. When this metaphorical fire burns steadily, traditions describe a person who feels capable, decisive, and able to act on their own behalf. When it burns too low, the same traditions describe self-doubt, passivity, or a tendency to defer. When it flares too high, the pattern shifts toward control, frustration, or pushing too hard.

Modern readers do not need to accept the chakra system literally to find the framework useful. At a psychological level, the Solar Plexus maps onto something most people recognize: the felt sense of confidence in the gut, the place where motivation gathers or stalls, the bodily location we point to when we say "trust your gut." The yellow color traditionally linked to this chakra mirrors warmth and daylight, which is why warm-toned stones, from golden Citrine to amber-brown Tiger Eye, are so often paired with it.

Working with this center, whether through crystals, breath, movement, or simple goal-setting, is less about "opening" something mystical and more about paying attention to how you relate to your own agency. The crystals below are tools people have long reached for at moments when they wanted to feel steadier, braver, or more clearly themselves.

Quick facts: Sanskrit Manipura · Position: Upper abdomen · Color: Yellow · Element: Fire. Balanced: Self-confidence, motivation, agency. Out of balance: Low self-worth, need for control, digestive tension.

Not sure which chakra to focus on? Take the free Chakra Test ↗

12 Best Crystals for the Solar Plexus Chakra

Tiger Eye

Chakra: Solar Plexus, Root, Sacral

Color: Golden brown (also blue/red)

Element: Fire

Best for: Confidence, Willpower, Grounding

Best way to use: Carry or hold in meditation

Zodiac: Leo, Capricorn

Affirmation: I trust my own will and act on it.

A variety of quartz (SiO₂) crossed with fibers of crocidolite, Tiger Eye gets its shifting golden-brown bands from a process called pseudomorphism, where one mineral slowly replaces another while keeping its fibrous shape. Roll a bead under a lamp and the chatoyant band glides like a cat's eye, a tactile cue of focus and steadiness. In Solar Plexus work it has long been used as a stone of measured confidence, the kind that does not need to shout, paired with a grounding Root connection that keeps willpower from tipping into pushiness. Many people carry it when facing a hard conversation or a decision they keep postponing.

Read full Tiger Eye meaning →

Carnelian

Chakra: Sacral, Solar Plexus, Root

Color: Orange, red-orange, reddish-brown

Element: Fire

Best for: Confidence, Starting projects, Creative work

Best way to use: Wear as jewelry

Zodiac: Aries, Taurus, Virgo

Affirmation: I have the energy to begin.

A chalcedony variety of SiO₂, Carnelian owes its warm red-orange to trace iron oxide, and many stones on the market have been heat-treated to deepen that color. Smooth and slightly cool at first touch, it warms quickly in the hand, a physical echo of the activation it represents. Linked to both Sacral creativity and Solar Plexus drive, it is the stone people reach for when they have ideas but no momentum, when the gap between intending and doing feels too wide. A small piece worn at the wrist is a gentle, daily reminder that starting is allowed.

Read full Carnelian meaning →

Citrine

Chakra: Solar Plexus, Sacral

Color: Yellow, golden-orange, brownish-yellow

Element: Fire

Best for: Wealth, Confidence, Morning energy

Best way to use: Wear as jewelry

Zodiac: Aries, Leo

Affirmation: I am worthy of good things.

Citrine is the yellow to golden-orange form of quartz (SiO₂), its color coming from trace iron heated within the earth (or, for most commercial stones, by careful treatment of amethyst or smoky quartz). Natural Citrine is rare and usually pale; the deeper cognac tones are almost always treated, which is worth knowing when buying. Traditionally tied to the Solar Plexus and to abundance imagery, it carries a clean, sunny warmth rather than a heavy one. For someone whose self-worth runs low in the morning, a Citrine piece worn or set on the bedside is a small, repeated note that the day is permitted to go well.

Read full Citrine meaning →

Malachite

Chakra: Heart, Solar Plexus

Color: Green (banded)

Element: Fire

Best for: Personal growth, Emotional release, Transitions

Best way to use: Wear as jewelry

Zodiac: Scorpio, Capricorn

Affirmation: I release what no longer fits me.

A copper carbonate mineral, Malachite forms those vivid concentric green bands through layered growth in cracks of limestone, the same banding that makes each piece unmistakable. Its surface takes a soft polish that feels almost waxy under the thumb. Bridging Heart and Solar Plexus, it is the stone people turn to when growth asks something of them, when old patterns of control or self-criticism need to loosen so something truer can take their place. Handle raw Malachite with care, keep it away from prolonged water soaking, and wash your hands after touching rough specimens.

Read full Malachite meaning →

Pyrite

Chakra: Solar Plexus

Color: Brass-yellow, metallic

Element: Fire

Best for: Motivation, Wealth mindset, Focus

Best way to use: Carry or hold in meditation

Zodiac: Leo

Affirmation: I act on what matters to me.

Iron disulfide (FeS₂), Pyrite's mirror-bright cubes and brass-yellow gleam come from its cubic crystal habit, and its nickname "fool's gold" is honest about how easily the eye mistakes it for the real thing. Cool, dense, and weighty for its size, it has a grounded feel that is part of its appeal. Strongly tied to the Solar Plexus, Pyrite is used when motivation is flat and follow-through is weak, when you need a clearer sense that your effort can amount to something. A single cube on a desk often serves as a quiet anchor for focused work.

Read full Pyrite meaning →

adamite

Chakra: Solar Plexus, Heart

Color: Yellow-green, yellow, white, pink

Element: Fire

Best for: Mood lift, Creative work, Display

Best way to use: Place in your space

Zodiac: Leo

Affirmation: I let myself feel glad to be here.

A zinc arsenate mineral, Adamite grows in radiant clusters of small yellow to yellow-green crystals that catch light from many angles at once, a piece genuinely nice to look at on a shelf. Its color comes from trace copper or manganese, and good specimens have a bright, almost lemony glow. Bridging Solar Plexus and Heart, it is often used as a gentle mood-lifter for days when drive and joy feel disconnected, when the will is working but the heart is not in it. Place it where you will see it in passing, not locked away in a box.

Read full adamite meaning →

aegirine

Chakra: Root, Sacral, Solar Plexus

Color: Dark green to black

Element: Earth, Fire

Best for: Protection, Boundaries, Breakthrough

Best way to use: Carry or hold in meditation

Zodiac: Aries, Scorpio, Capricorn

Affirmation: I hold my ground and stay clear.

A sodium iron silicate, Aegirine forms long, blade-like prismatic crystals in dark green to near-black, a striking contrast to the warm yellows that dominate this chakra. The bladed habit is literal structure you can feel between your fingers. Spanning Root, Sacral, and Solar Plexus, it is used when confidence needs a backbone of protection first, when you cannot feel your power because you do not yet feel safe enough to express it. A grounded stone for people working on boundaries they have struggled to keep.

Read full aegirine meaning →

amber

Chakra: Sacral, Solar Plexus

Color: Yellow, Orange, Brown, Honey

Element: Fire

Best for: Warmth, Protection, Comfort

Best way to use: Wear as jewelry

Zodiac: Leo, Aquarius

Affirmation: I carry my own warmth with me.

Amber is not a mineral at all but fossilized tree resin, often 30 to 90 million years old, that slowly hardened into a soft, lightweight, warm-to-the-touch material. Its honey yellows and oranges come from microscopic bubbles and trapped air, and occasionally it holds an insect or plant fragment that pre-dates human beings by epochs. Tied to Sacral and Solar Plexus warmth, it is worn as comfort and protection, a small piece of ancient sunlight against the skin. Light enough for a pendant you forget you are wearing until you need the reminder.

Read full amber meaning →

ametrine

Chakra: Solar Plexus, Crown, Third Eye

Color: Purple and gold (bicolor)

Element: Air, Fire

Best for: Focus, Creativity, Confidence

Best way to use: Wear as jewelry

Zodiac: Libra, Pisces, Gemini

Affirmation: I trust my clarity and act on it.

A natural bicolor of Amethyst and Citrine within a single quartz crystal (SiO₂), Ametrine forms when uneven heat during growth leaves part of the iron in its purple state and part in its golden state, most commercial supply coming from one mine in Bolivia. Holding one stone with two colors makes its symbolism easy to grasp: clarity of mind paired with warmth of will. Used across Solar Plexus, Third Eye, and Crown, it suits people whose confidence falters not from lack of drive but from overthinking, the doer and the doubter in the same stone.

Read full ametrine meaning →

andalusite

Chakra: Solar Plexus, Third Eye

Color: Olive green, brown, reddish (pleochroic)

Element: Earth

Best for: Decisions, Perspective, Fair judgment

Best way to use: Carry or hold in meditation

Zodiac: Virgo, Libra

Affirmation: I see more than one side and still choose.

An aluminum silicate mineral, Andalusite is pleochroic, meaning it shows different colors (olive, brown, reddish) from different angles without any treatment at all, the shift is built into the crystal structure. Turn a cut stone in your hand and the hues trade places, a useful metaphor for its traditional use. Linked to Solar Plexus and Third Eye, it is reached for at decision points, when confidence needs to be paired with perspective rather than speed. A quiet stone for people who must weigh competing claims and still move forward.

Read full andalusite meaning →

arsenopyrite

Chakra: Root, Solar Plexus

Color: Silvery white metallic

Element: Earth, Fire

Best for: Protection, Transformation, Breaking Chains

Best way to use: Wear, carry, or place in your space

Zodiac: Scorpio, Capricorn

Affirmation: I let go of what held me back.

An iron arsenic sulfide mineral, Arsenopyrite forms hard, silvery, metallic crystals often found alongside gold ores, which is part of its lore of transformation, the dull rock that hides the treasure. Cool and dense with a sharp metallic gleam, it has a no-nonsense feel in the hand. Spanning Root and Solar Plexus, it is used for the harder side of personal power work, breaking old patterns, endings, and the slow business of becoming someone who no longer fits an old role. Handle with normal care for any sulfide mineral, and keep it out of prolonged moisture.

Read full arsenopyrite meaning →

baltic-amber

Chakra: Solar Plexus, Sacral

Color: Pale yellow, honey, cognac, cherry

Element: Fire

Best for: Mood lift, Comfort, Optimism

Best way to use: Wear as jewelry

Zodiac: Leo, Aquarius

Affirmation: I let in light, even on hard days.

Baltic Amber is fossilized resin from ancient pinewood forests that grew around the Baltic Sea basin roughly 35 to 50 million years ago, and it carries a higher succinic acid content than amber from most other regions, which is why it is so prized. Pale lemon to deep cognac, sometimes cherry-red, it is feather-light and warm against the skin within seconds. Tied to Solar Plexus and Sacral mood and comfort, it is the piece parents often choose for children and adults choose for themselves on low-energy days, gentle, wearable, undemanding warmth.

Read full baltic-amber meaning →

How to Choose by Your Solar Plexus Need

Different Solar Plexus struggles call for different stones. Use this table to match what you are actually feeling with the crystal traditionally used for that need.

NeedBest CrystalsWhy
Confidence & self-worthCitrine, Tiger Eye, PyriteWarm yellow and golden tones long linked to a steady sense of personal value and visibility
Motivation & follow-throughCarnelian, Pyrite, Tiger EyeFire-element stones used when drive is low and starting feels harder than it should be
Decision & clarityAmetrine, Andalusite, Tiger EyeStones that pair Solar Plexus will with clearer seeing, useful at crossroads
Boundaries & protectionAegirine, Arsenopyrite, MalachiteDarker, grounding stones for when you cannot feel your power because safety comes first
Mood lift & comfortAmber, Baltic Amber, AdamiteLightweight, warm, sunny stones for low days when drive and joy feel far apart
Transformation & releaseMalachite, Arsenopyrite, AegirineStones used for endings, growth edges, and the work of becoming someone new

How to Use Solar Plexus Crystals

Solar Plexus work is less about the right stone and more about repeated, simple contact. A crystal you actually use will always outperform one that sits in a drawer.

Wear it. A bracelet, pendant, or ring keeps a warm-toned stone within view through the day. Carnelian, Citrine, Amber, and Baltic Amber suit jewelry well because they are light and take a good polish. Wear something on the non-dominant wrist, or as a pendant that rests near the upper abdomen, the traditional location of this chakra.

Hold it in meditation. Five to ten minutes is enough. Sit upright, place a Tiger Eye, Pyrite, or Citrine in both palms, and breathe slowly into the upper belly. Picture a warm yellow glow behind your navel that brightens a little with each exhale. If your mind wanders, the stone's weight gives you something to return to.

Place it on the body. Lying down, rest the stone an inch or two above the navel, over the upper abdomen. This is the Solar Plexus position. Stay for ten to fifteen minutes, breathing gently. Malachite and Pyrite are commonly used this way, placed flat rather than pointed.

Place it in your space. A cube of Pyrite on the desk where you do focused work, a cluster of Adamite where you will see it in passing, a piece of Tiger Eye near the door you leave through each morning. Placement turns the stone into a quiet, repeated cue.

Where to place (body): the upper abdomen, between the navel and the sternum, is the Solar Plexus point. For meditation or body placement, that is the spot. For daily wear, anywhere you will notice the stone works just as well.

Beyond Crystals: Other Ways to Balance the Solar Plexus

Crystals are one tool among many. The Solar Plexus responds especially well to simple, repeatable practices that build a felt sense of agency in the body. You do not need all of these, pick one or two and let them settle in.

  • Core breathwork. The Solar Plexus sits in the same region as the diaphragm. Slow belly breathing, four counts in, six counts out, for three to five minutes, directly engages this area and is one of the fastest ways to settle an overcharged will or wake a sluggish one.
  • Morning sunlight. Five to ten minutes of daylight within an hour of waking helps set the daily rhythm the Solar Plexus is so tied to. Step outside, face the light, breathe. This is the bodily equivalent of Citrine's warmth.
  • Warrior pose. In yoga, Virabhadrasana (Warrior I and II) is the posture most linked to this chakra, the stance of someone who can hold their ground. A few minutes of warrior pose, feet rooted, arms strong, gaze steady, gives the body a direct experience of personal power.
  • Goal setting, small and real. Pick one small action you can finish today and finish it. The Solar Plexus is built on completed follow-through, not grand plans. Repeated small wins teach the nervous system that your will counts.
  • Yellow and warm-toned foods. The color link is traditional rather than evidence-based, but warm, nourishing foods, ginger, turmeric, oats, corn, banana, lemon, and yellow peppers, fit the same steadying, comforting register as the stones on this list.

None of these are treatments for medical conditions. They are small, repeatable ways of paying attention to how you relate to your own drive and self-worth.

FAQ

Where do I place a Solar Plexus crystal on my body?

On the upper abdomen, between the navel and the bottom of the sternum. Lie down, place the stone flat an inch or two above the navel, and rest for ten to fifteen minutes while breathing slowly into the belly.

How do I cleanse Solar Plexus crystals?

Rinse in cool running water and dry with a soft cloth, or place on a selenite plate overnight. Avoid prolonged soaking for Malachite and Pyrite, and keep Amber away from heat and harsh chemicals, which can dull its surface. A few hours of gentle morning light also works, but avoid long direct sun for Citrine and Ametrine, which can fade.

Which crystal is best for confidence?

Tiger Eye, Citrine, and Carnelian are the most commonly recommended. Tiger Eye for steady, grounded confidence; Citrine for warmth and self-worth; Carnelian for the energy to actually begin. Choose the one whose feel and color you respond to.

Can I wear Solar Plexus crystals every day?

Yes. Carnelian, Citrine, Amber, and Baltic Amber are light enough for daily jewelry. Rotate stones every few weeks if one starts to feel routine; the value is in attention, not in any single piece.

How do I know if my Solar Plexus is out of balance?

Common signs people describe are low self-worth, difficulty making decisions, a need to control situations or other people, digestive tension, or a flat, stuck feeling about goals. A combination of several of these is a clearer signal than any one alone.

Are the effects of Solar Plexus crystals scientifically proven?

No. The benefits described here come from tradition and from people's reported experience, not from clinical proof. Crystals are widely used as complementary wellness and mindfulness tools, not as treatments for anxiety, illness, or any medical condition. If you are struggling with persistent low mood, worry, or self-worth, a qualified mental-health professional is the right first step.

Can I combine Solar Plexus crystals with stones for other chakras?

Yes. Common pairings are Solar Plexus with Root for grounding, or Solar Plexus with Heart when confidence needs to stay connected to care. Ametrine already spans Solar Plexus and the upper chakras in a single stone.

Do yellow crystals only work for the Solar Plexus?

Not at all. Color is a traditional guide, not a rule. Pyrite is metallic, Aegirine is dark green to black, and Malachite is green, yet all are used for this chakra. Choose by the need described in the table above, not by color alone.
